sections.label: Section 600
sections.original: οἶσθ ’ ὡς μέτευξαι , καὶ σοφωτέρα φανῇ ;
sections.translation: Change that prayer as I will teach thee , and thou wilt show more wisdom . Never let happiness appear in sorrow ’ s guise , nor , when thy fortune smiles , pretend she frowns !

sections.label: Section 603
sections.original: ὕβριζ ’, ἐπειδὴ σοὶ μὲν ἔστ ’ ἀποστροφή ,
sections.translation: Mock on ; thou hast a place of refuge ; I am alone , an exile soon to be .

sections.label: Section 605
sections.original: αὐτὴ τάδ ’ εἵλου · μηδέν ’ ἄλλον αἰτιῶ .
sections.translation: Thy own free choice was this ; blame no one else .

sections.label: Section 606
sections.original: τί δρῶσα ; μῶν γαμοῦσα καὶ προδοῦσά σε ;
sections.translation: What did I do ? Marry , then betray thee ?

sections.label: Section 607
sections.original: ἀρὰς τυράννοις ἀνοσίους ἀρωμένη .
sections.translation: Against the king thou didst invoke an impious curse .

sections.label: Section 608
sections.original: καὶ σοῖς ἀραία γ ’ οὖσα τυγχάνω δόμοις .
sections.translation: On thy house too maybe I bring the curse .

sections.label: Section 609
sections.original: ὡς οὐ κρινοῦμαι τῶνδέ σοι τὰ πλείονα .
sections.translation: Know this , I will no further dispute this point with thee .

sections.label: Section 610
sections.original: ἀλλ ’, εἴ τι βούλῃ παισὶν ἢ σαυτῆς φυγῇ
sections.translation: But , if thou wilt of my fortune somewhat take for the children or thyself to help thy exile , say on ; for I am ready to grant it with ungrudging hand , yea and to send tokens to my friends elsewhere who shall treat thee well . If thou refuse this offer , thou wilt do a foolish deed ,

sections.label: Section 615
sections.original: λήξασα δ ’ ὀργῆς κερδανεῖς ἀμείνονα .
sections.translation: but if thou cease from anger the greater will be thy gain .

sections.label: Section 616
sections.original: οὔτ ’ ἂν ξένοισι τοῖσι σοῖς χρησαίμεθ ’ ἄν ,
sections.translation: I will have naught to do with friends of thine , naught will I receive of thee , offer it not to me ; a villain ’ s gifts can bring no blessing .

sections.label: Section 619
sections.original: ἀλλ ’ οὖν ἐγὼ μὲν δαίμονας μαρτύρομαι ,
sections.translation: At least I call the gods to witness ,

sections.label: Section 620
sections.original: ὡς πάνθ ’ ὑπουργεῖν σοί τε καὶ τέκνοις θέλω ·
sections.translation: that I am ready in all things to serve thee and thy children , but thou dost scorn my favours and thrustest thy friends stubbornly away ; wherefore thy lot will be more bitter still .

sections.label: Section 623
sections.original: χώρει · πόθῳ γὰρ τῆς νεοδμήτου κόρης
sections.translation: Away ! By love for thy young bride entrapped , too long thou lingerest outside her chamber ;

sections.label: Section 625
sections.original: νύμφευ ’· ἴσως γάρ — σὺν θεῷ δ ’ εἰρήσεται —
sections.translation: go wed , for , if God will , thou shalt have such a marriage as thou wouldst fain refuse .

sections.label: Section 1.627
sections.original: ἔρωτες ὑπὲρ μὲν ἄγαν
sections.translation: When in excess and past all limits Love doth come , he brings not glory or repute

sections.label: Section 1.630
sections.original: ἀνδράσιν · εἰ δ ’ ἅλις ἔλθοι
sections.translation: to man ; but if the Cyprian queen in moderate might approach , no goddess is so full of charm as she . Never , O never , lady mine , discharge at me from thy golden bow a shaft invincible , in passion ’ s venom dipped .

sections.label: Section 1.635
sections.original: στέργοι δέ με σωφροσύνα ,
sections.translation: On me may chastity , heaven ’ s fairest gift , look [*] with a favouring eye ;

sections.label: Section 1.640
sections.original: προσβάλοι δεινὰ Κύπρις , ἀπτολέμους δ ’ εὐνὰς σεβίζουσ ’
sections.translation: never may Cypris , goddess dread , fasten on me a temper to dispute , or restless jealousy , smiting my soul with mad desire for unlawful love , but may she hallow peaceful married life and shrewdly decide whom each of us shall wed .

sections.label: Section 2.643
sections.original: ὦ πατρίς , ὦ δώματα , μὴ
sections.translation: O my country , O my own dear home ! God grant I may never be an outcast from my city ,

sections.label: Section 2.645
sections.original: τὸν ἀμηχανίας ἔχουσα
sections.translation: leading that cruel helpless life , whose every day is misery . Ere that may I this life complete and yield to death , ay , death ;

sections.label: Section 2.650
sections.original: χθων δ ’ οὐκ ἄλλος ὕπερθεν ἢ
sections.translation: for there is no misery that doth surpass the loss of fatherland .

sections.label: Section 2.652
sections.original: εἴδομεν , οὐκ ἐξ ἑτέρων
sections.translation: I have seen with mine eyes , nor from the lips of others have I the lesson learnt ;

sections.label: Section 2.655
sections.original: σὲ γὰρ οὐ πόλις , οὐ φίλων τις
sections.translation: no city , not one friend doth pity thee in this thine awful woe . May he perish and find no favour ,

sections.label: Section 2.660
sections.original: μὴ φίλους τιμᾶν καθαρᾶν ἀνοί-
sections.translation: whoso hath not in him honour for his friends , freely unlocking his heart to them . Never shall he be friend of mine .

sections.label: Section 663
sections.original: Μήδεια , χαῖρε · τοῦδε γὰρ προοίμιον
sections.translation: All hail , Medea ! no man knoweth fairer prelude to the greeting of friends than this .

sections.label: Section 665
sections.original: ὦ χαῖρε καὶ σύ , παῖ σοφοῦ Πανδίονος ,
sections.translation: All hail to thee likewise , Aegeus , son of wise Pandion . Whence comest thou to this land ?

sections.label: Section 667
sections.original: Φοίβου παλαιὸν ἐκλιπὼν χρηστήριον .
sections.translation: From Phoebus ’ ancient oracle .

sections.label: Section 668
sections.original: τί δ ’ ὀμφαλὸν γῆς θεσπιῳδὸν ἐστάλης ;
sections.translation: What took thee on thy travels to the prophetic centre of the earth ?

sections.label: Section 669
sections.original: παίδων ἐρευνῶν σπέρμ ’ ὅπως γένοιτό μοι .
sections.translation: The wish to ask how I might raise up seed unto myself .

sections.label: Section 670
sections.original: πρὸς θεῶν — ἄπαις γὰρ δεῦρ ’ ἀεὶ τείνεις βίον ;
sections.translation: Pray tell me , hast thou till now dragged on a childless life ?

sections.label: Section 671
sections.original: ἄπαιδές ἐσμεν δαίμονός τινος τύχῃ .
sections.translation: I have no child owing to the visitation of some god .

sections.label: Section 672
sections.original: δάμαρτος οὔσης , ἢ λέχους ἄπειρος ὤν ;
sections.translation: Hast thou a wife , or hast thou never known the married state ?

sections.label: Section 673
sections.original: οὐκ ἐσμὲν εὐνῆς ἄζυγες γαμηλίου .
sections.translation: I have a wife joined to me in wedlock ’ s bond .

sections.label: Section 674
sections.original: τί δῆτα Φοῖβος εἶπέ σοι παίδων πέρι ;
sections.translation: What said Phoebus to thee as to children ?

sections.label: Section 675
sections.original: σοφώτερ ’ ἢ κατ ’ ἄνδρα συμβαλεῖν ἔπη .
sections.translation: Words too subtle for man to comprehend .

sections.label: Section 676
sections.original: θέμις μὲν ἡμᾶς χρησμὸν εἰδέναι θεοῦ ;
sections.translation: Surely I may learn the god ’ s answer ?

sections.label: Section 677
sections.original: μάλιστ ’, ἐπεί τοι καὶ σοφῆς δεῖται φρενός .
sections.translation: Most assuredly , for it is just thy subtle wit it needs .

sections.label: Section 678
sections.original: τί δῆτ ’ ἔχρησε ; λέξον , εἰ θέμις κλύειν .
sections.translation: What said the god ? speak , if I may hear it .

sections.label: Section 679
sections.original: ἀσκοῦ με τὸν προύχοντα μὴ λῦσαι πόδα —
sections.translation: He bade me not loose the wineskin ’ s pendent neck . [*]

sections.label: Section 680
sections.original: πρὶν ἂν τί δράσῃς ἢ τίν ’ ἐξίκῃ χθόνα ;
sections.translation: Till when ? what must thou do first , what country visit ?

sections.label: Section 681
sections.original: πρὶν ἂν πατρῴαν αὖθις ἑστίαν μόλω .
sections.translation: Till I to my native home return .

sections.label: Section 682
sections.original: σὺ δ ’ ὡς τί χρῄζων τήνδε ναυστολεῖς χθόνα ;
sections.translation: What object hast thou in sailing to this land ?

sections.label: Section 683
sections.original: Πιτθεύς τις ἔστι , γῆς ἄναξ Tροζηνίας . . . .
sections.translation: O ’ er Troezen ’ s realm is Pittheus king .

sections.label: Section 684
sections.original: παῖς , ὡς λέγουσι , Πέλοπος , εὐσεβέστατος .
sections.translation: Pelops ’ son , a man devout they say .

sections.label: Section 685
sections.original: τούτῳ θεοῦ μάντευμα κοινῶσαι θέλω .
sections.translation: To him I fain would impart the oracle of the god .

sections.label: Section 686
sections.original: σοφὸς γὰρ ἁνὴρ καὶ τρίβων τὰ τοιάδε .
sections.translation: The man is shrewd and versed in such-like lore .

sections.label: Section 687
sections.original: κἀμοί γε πάντων φίλτατος δορυξένων .
sections.translation: Aye , and to me the dearest of all my warrior friends .

sections.label: Section 688
sections.original: ἀλλ ’ εὐτυχοίης καὶ τύχοις ὅσων ἐρᾷς .
sections.translation: Good luck to thee ! success to all thy wishes !

sections.label: Section 689
sections.original: τί γὰρ σὸν ὄμμα χρώς τε συντέτηχ ’ ὅδε ;
sections.translation: But why that downcast eye , that wasted cheek ?

sections.label: Section 690
sections.original: Αἰγεῦ , κάκιστός ἐστί μοι πάντων πόσις .
sections.translation: O Aegeus , my husband has proved a monster of iniquity .

sections.label: Section 691
sections.original: τί φῄς ; σαφῶς μοι σὰς φράσον δυσθυμίας .
sections.translation: What meanest thou ? explain to me clearly the cause of thy despondency .

sections.label: Section 692
sections.original: ἀδικεῖ μ ’ Ἰάσων οὐδὲν ἐξ ἐμοῦ παθών .
sections.translation: Jason is wronging me though I have given him no cause .

sections.label: Section 693
sections.original: τί χρῆμα δράσας ; φράζε μοι σαφέστερον .
sections.translation: What hath he done ? tell me more clearly .

sections.label: Section 694
sections.original: γυναῖκ ’ ἐφ ’ ἡμῖν δεσπότιν δόμων ἔχει .
sections.translation: He is taking another wife to succeed me as mistress of his house .

sections.label: Section 695
sections.original: οὔ που τετόλμηκ ’ ἔργον αἴσχιστον τόδε ;
sections.translation: Can he have brought himself to such a dastard deed ?

sections.label: Section 696
sections.original: σάφ ’ ἴσθ ’· ἄτιμοι δ ’ ἐσμὲν οἱ πρὸ τοῦ φίλοι .
sections.translation: Be assured thereof ; I , whom he loved of yore , am in dishonour now .

sections.label: Section 697
sections.original: πότερον ἐρασθεὶς ἢ σὸν ἐχθαίρων λέχος ;
sections.translation: Hath he found a new love ? or does he loathe thy bed ?

sections.label: Section 698
sections.original: μέγαν γ ’ ἔρωτα πιστὸς οὐκ ἔφυ φίλοις .
sections.translation: Much in love is he ! A traitor to his friend is he become .

sections.label: Section 699
sections.original: ἴτω νυν , εἴπερ , ὡς λέγεις , ἐστὶν κακός .
sections.translation: Enough ! if he is a villain as thou sayest .

sections.label: Section 700
sections.original: ἀνδρῶν τυράννων κῆδος ἠράσθη λαβεῖν .
sections.translation: The alliance he is so much enamoured of is with a princess .
